comments.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
comments.text: If you moved here tomorrow, what is the ONE thing you would bring with you? I’m reading every comment below! 👇

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
📌 Join the journey with us! 
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VByI581slJc&list=PLPsFFj_F_V2ESDcWWCFwfHwlojUqv2I8E
We are uncovering the world's best-kept secrets, one village at a time. Are you coming with us on the next one?

comments.author: @Gongsanpal
comments.text: I learned that Norway is very calm and beautiful country by this UTube video.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@suthakar5065 
Thank you so much for the love from India! We're glad you enjoyed exploring this Norwegian village with us.

comments.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
comments.text: The scenery is breathtaking, but what stayed with me most wasn't the fjords—it was the idea that real wealth might simply be having enough time, enough trust, and enough peace. Which part of Norway's lifestyle impressed you the most?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@RemoteEarthJourneys 
Thank you for such a thoughtful comment! You’ve captured the essence of the Nordic soul perfectly—true wealth is indeed found in peace and community trust. While this film features the Faroe Islands, what impressed me most about the lifestyle there is the 'slow living' philosophy and their deep connection to nature. It’s a powerful reminder that we often need much less than we think to be happy.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: Thank you! We’re glad the intro caught your attention. Hope you enjoy the rest of the documentary!

comments.author: @rickwhite4137
comments.text:
🚫 *_STOP THIS NONSENSE!_* 🚫 There is no tundra on mainland Norway! Tundra is found on Svalbard, much farther north. 
"Winter lasts for months." So what? Winter in Norway isn't that harsh, except for some places high in the mountains and Finnmarksvidda in the north. People can easily handle it!
I hate these videos where the main point is to scare people rather than educate them. But it's not possible to educate people when the storyteller has absolutely no idea and makes up things along the way.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@rickwhite4137
Thank you for your feedback! We appreciate the correction and will keep it in mind for our future documentaries!

comments.author: @MaewynSucat
comments.text: It seems like everything is man made!!❤🎉

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: I had the opposite feeling—the way people build around the landscape instead of trying to dominate it is what impressed me most. Which place in the video made you feel that way?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@MaewynSucat 
Thank you! It’s truly fascinating how these traditional wooden houses blend so harmoniously with the rugged natural beauty of Norway. Glad you enjoyed the film!

comments.author: @sailingviking7885
comments.text: The quiet and peaceful Norway described in this video will soon be a thing of the past. Norwegian politicians' choice to incorporate Norway into the EU via the EEA and the Schengen Agreement has meant that Norway has been invaded by fortune hunters and foreign cultures that do not want to be assimilated. This means that what has been a distinct Norwegian culture is now changing so drastically that within 50 - 100 years it will only be found in history books. The worst thing is that even ethnic Norwegian youth have already lost their connection to the history that created the Norwegian culture that this video so romantically describes. Yes, the fantastic Norwegian nature will exist but the culture will soon be gone for good. Sad but true!

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: @frodemarkhus3692 We’d appreciate the feedback to help us improve.

replies.author: @frodemarkhus3692
replies.text: 100% bulshitt.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@sailingviking7885 
Thank you for sharing your perspective. While our focus is on capturing the current beauty and atmosphere of these locations, we understand that societies and cultures are constantly evolving. We hope this documentary serves as a meaningful record of the Norway we see and appreciate today.

comments.author: @BelleRuusu
comments.text: Its not Norway btw is the happiest country, it is Finland! But ofcourse Norway and Switzerland are the richest country in Europe!

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: You're right that Finland has topped the World Happiness Report in recent years. Norway is consistently near the top as well, so it's fascinating how the Nordic countries seem to share many of the same values around trust, balance, and quality of life.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: You’re absolutely right! Finland currently holds the top spot, though Norway consistently ranks as one of the happiest and wealthiest nations. Both are truly incredible places. Thanks for watching and sharing your insights!

comments.author: @sanjaymeriya5224
comments.text: Remembering the days and time when I was working for Cruise and most of the cherishing itineraries I did in Norway, one of my favorites across the globe. ❤

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: That must have been an unforgettable chapter of your life. Seeing Norway from the sea again and again probably lets you notice details most travelers never get to experience—which itinerary still feels the most magical to you?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@sanjaymeriya5224 
That sounds like such an incredible experience! Norway truly has a unique magic that stays with you forever. Thank you so much for sharing your beautiful memories with us!

comments.author: @HappyRuby18
comments.text: I'll go there for holiday soon, Amithofo🙏🙏🙏

replies.author: @HappyRuby18
replies.text: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe Thank you so much Bro 😍Please wish me a smooth and enjoyable trip to Norway🙏

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: @HappyRuby18 You're very welcome! I hope you have an unforgettable adventure in Norway. Feel free to come back and share your favorite moments with us later! 🙏✨

replies.author: @HappyRuby18
replies.text: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe Thank you so much🙏🙏

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@HappyRuby18 
That sounds like an amazing plan! I hope you have a wonderful trip and enjoy the stunning scenery of Norway. Safe travels! 🙏

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text: @susettefernandez941 Thank you! Glad you noticed!

comments.author: @forgottencampingsecrets
comments.text: if i were to move here tommorrow best believe i'm bringing my blunt. that is if it's legal, ofcourse.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@forgottencampingsecrets 
With views this breathtaking, the fresh Arctic air is probably the only 'high' you’ll ever need! But definitely double-check those local laws first. Glad you enjoyed the documentary!

comments.author: @johnmarkmaille9589
comments.text: the most importent in life is caring

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@johnmarkmaille9589 
Thank you so much for your thoughtful comment! You are absolutely right—caring for one another and our world is what truly gives life meaning, especially in close-knit communities like the ones we featured in this documentary.

replies.author: @RemoteEarthJourneys
replies.text: Absolutely! It feels like the ingredients speak for themselves there—the seafood looked so fresh that even the simplest dishes seemed unforgettable. Have you ever tried Norwegian salmon or stockfish?

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@johnmarkmaille9589 
Thank you! One of the best parts of living in these Norwegian fishing villages is definitely having access to such incredibly fresh seafood.

replies.author: @ThePlanetJournalGlobe
replies.text:
@johnmarkmaille9589 
Thank you! Their strong sense of community and unity is exactly what makes life in these remote fishing villages so special and resilient.
